overview: From 2026-06-04 to 2026-06-05, elizaos/eliza had 39 new PRs (18 merged), 3 new issues, and 8 active contributors.

topIssues.title: orchestrator: sub-agent completion narration drops/garbles the deliverable (narration-envelope)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

When the planner narrates a coding sub-agent's `task_complete` back to the user, the **deliverable** (the printed value / command output) is frequently dropped, truncated, or buried under process chatter — while the underlying sub-agent work is correct. This is a *presentation* defect, not a capability one.

Evidence: a live 48-prompt sweep (30-scenario battery + smart/normie/harden suites) across all three backends found **0 capability-broken defects** — every answer was correct, every spawn produced its artifact, every safety probe handled. The **only** remaining defect class is this narration-envelope.

## Manifestations (all observed live)

1. **Deliverable drop on combo / printed-value tasks.** On the no-change-set completion path, `composeNarration` → `stripToolTranscript(response)` (`sub-agent-router.ts`) deletes the entire `[tool output: …] … [/tool output]` block. For a "print a value / run a command and report the output" task (no files changed → no diff), that block *is* the deliverable, so it's gone before the user sees it.
2. **Value truncation by the parent model.** The synthetic `task_complete` memory is re-rendered by the parent `RESPONSE_HANDLER` (a weak planner in some deployments), which truncates the value — e.g. `2026-06-02` → `202`, a BTC price `70234` → `705`. Per-backend signature: opencode truncates, codex emits verbose process narration + `Completed <task>` banners, claude is cleanest.
3. **Stale retry attempts leak.** When a first attempt errors (e.g. `session_state_lost` / an ACP crash) and a respawn recovers, the user sees *both* `"the sub-agent was blocked / crashed, let me try again"` **and** the eventual deliverable.
4. **Process chatter.** Sub-agents (esp. codex) narrate their steps (`"I'll load the workspace context first"`, `"rg is not installed"`, `"reverse.py already exists"`); the parent relays it.

## Proposed structural fixes

**(a) Carry the printed-value deliverable verbatim (fixes #1, mitigates #2).** A structural carrier that mirrors the existing verified-URL carrier:
- *Producer* (`sub-agent-router.ts`): on the `task_complete && !changeSet && verifiedUrls.length === 0` path, recover the captured stdout from the **raw** `response` (before `composeNarration` strips it) via the inverse of `stripToolTranscript`, and stamp it as `metadata.subAgentDeliverable` (gated to a short single block, ≤2KB, so multi-KB transcripts stay on the model-rendered path). *(I have a ready, tested producer patch for this.)*
- *Consumer* (`evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts`): when `subAgentDeliverable` is present, relay it **verbatim** instead of letting the model re-summarize/truncate it — parallel to how `subAgentVerifiedUrls` is already consumed.

**(b) Terminal-reply filter (fixes #3, #4).** Emit only the *terminal* sub-agent reply; suppress stale `blocked`/`failed`/`state_lost` attempts that a later retry superseded, plus raw tool chatter and `Completed <task>` banners. **Caveat — needs care:** the `session_state_lost` error event is currently *coupled to the respawn trigger* (re-injecting it is what prompts the parent to respawn), so suppressing the user-facing narration requires an internal-only routing path that keeps the respawn signal. This is the part that needs design discussion.

**(c) Already shipped — source-side mitigation.** #8158 adds an output-discipline section to the scaffolded sub-agent operating manual ("lead with the deliverable, don't narrate process, no `Completed <task>` banner, a bare workspace has no SOUL.md/USER.md — don't hunt for them"), which measurably reduced #4 at the source (codex re-probes went from a verbose banner to a bare `5050` / `2026-06-02`).

## Coordination note

`sub-agent-router.ts` and `evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts` were recently rewritten on `develop`; this should be coordinated with that work rather than landed blind. Happy to open the (a)-producer + (a)-consumer as a focused PR if the approach looks right.

topIssues.title: app-core dist/entry.js externalizes un-hoisted transitive deps (jose, @node-rs/argon2) → ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND outside Bun's loader
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

`packages/app-core`'s built `dist/entry.js` imports transitive runtime deps (`jose`, `@node-rs/argon2`, and likely more) as **external** packages. These deps live in Bun's content-addressable store but are not linked at the top level where Node-style resolution looks, so any command that runs `dist/entry.js` outside Bun's workspace-aware loader fails with `Cannot find module`.

Surfaced by a downstream QA smoke pass (milady-ai/milady#2179, finding #1 "layer 2").

## Repro

1. Build the app-core entry (`dist/entry.js`, ~7.6 MB across ~20 chunks).
2. Run it with a plain Node-style loader (e.g. via `doctor`, or any path that doesn't go through Bun's workspace loader).
3. First failure: `Cannot find module 'jose'`. Manually linking `jose` from Bun's store unblocks it, then it immediately hits `Cannot find module '@node-rs/argon2'`, and so on — whack-a-mole suggesting dozens more.

## Root cause

The bundler (tsdown) marks these runtime deps `external`, but they are transitive (not hoisted to the top-level `node_modules`), so Node-style resolution from `dist/entry.js` can't find them.

## Proposed fixes (pick one)

- (a) Bundle the needed transitive runtime deps **inline** in `dist/entry.js` (tsdown config: move them out of `external`).
- (b) Declare all needed runtime deps as **direct deps** of the package so they hoist to the top level.
- (c) Ship a runtime/loader that knows how to resolve into Bun's store.

## Severity

Medium — affects any command that runs `dist/entry.js` outside Bun's loader (e.g. `doctor`), and is broader than a single command.

topIssues.title: odysseus CalendarView: wire to the canonical LifeOps calendar API (2 decisions needed)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Context

The odysseus `CalendarView` (`plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/odysseus/CalendarView.tsx`) is currently **localStorage-backed** (keys `calendars` / `calendar-events`), and its header comment claims *"no frontend-callable client method exists to fetch/create events"* — **this is stale/wrong.** The full canonical stack exists and is live-mounted:

- **Types:** `LifeOpsCalendarEvent` / `LifeOpsCalendarFeed` / `CreateLifeOpsCalendarEventRequest` (`packages/shared/src/contracts/lifeops.ts`)
- **Service:** `LifeOpsCalendarService` (`plugins/plugin-lifeops/src/lifeops/service-mixin-calendar.ts`) over table `app_lifeops.life_calendar_events`
- **Routes (mounted):** `GET /api/lifeops/calendar/feed`, `/calendars`, `POST/PATCH/DELETE /calendar/events` (`plugins/plugin-lifeops/src/routes/lifeops-routes.ts`)
- **Client methods:** `client.getLifeOpsCalendarFeed / getLifeOpsCalendars / create|update|deleteLifeOpsCalendarEvent` (`plugins/plugin-lifeops/src/api/client-lifeops.ts`), augmented onto the `@elizaos/ui` `client`
- **Reference live consumer:** `useCalendarWeek.ts` → `LifeOpsCalendarSection.tsx`

So wiring CalendarView needs **no new API** — just reuse the canonical one, mirroring `useCalendarWeek`. But two decisions are yours to make before I open a PR:

### 1. Package boundary
`plugin-task-coordinator` depends only on `@elizaos/ui`, not `plugin-lifeops`; the calendar methods are attached as a **side-effect** of importing plugin-lifeops, so `client.getLifeOpsCalendarFeed` is `undefined` in the view bundle today.
- **Option A (smaller):** add `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops` dep + `import "@elizaos/plugin-lifeops/ui"` side-effect to the task-coordinator view entry.
- **Option B (first-class):** relocate the calendar client methods into the `@elizaos/ui` `api/` barrel (matches how orchestrator methods live in `client-agent.ts`). Cross-cutting @elizaos/ui change.

### 2. Product direction
`LifeOpsCalendarEvent` is a **Google/Apple-provider-backed** sync cache (writes 403 without a grant). odysseus CalendarView is **local-first** (free-form CRUD, per-calendar colors, `.ics` import/export). Should the wired CalendarView **replace** local-first with the provider feed, or **augment** it (provider feed + keep local calendars + .ics)?

## Proposed first slice (once decided)
Read-only: swap the `getLifeOpsCalendars` + `getLifeOpsCalendarFeed` load (mirror `useCalendarWeek`), keep `.ics`/quick-add honestly local/disabled, fix the stale header. Writes (create/update/delete) in a follow-up. PR-for-review, no surprise merge.

cc @shawmakesmagic — which option (A/B) + replace-vs-augment do you want? Then I'll ship the read-only slice.

topIssues.title: Storybook (packages/ui): feature-surface stories blocked — Vite dep-optimizer resolves @elizaos/core via its Node entry (pulls plugin-manager → fs-extra)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

In the `packages/ui` Storybook catalog, stories for **feature-surface components that transitively import `@elizaos/core`** cannot render in the browser preview. This tracks the root cause + fix options so the deferred stories can be added once the harness lands.

Scope: Storybook-harness follow-up for the work on branch `nubs/continuous-chat-overlay` (commits `0fceb71f9c`, `f4abd24189`). The purely-presentational stories already ship and render; this is only about the i18n / model-hub surfaces. Full write-up lives in `packages/ui/.storybook/README.md` (limitation #3).

## Affected stories (written, held back — not committed broken)

- `apps/RunningAppsRow`, `apps/AppIdentity`
- `local-inference/{ActiveModelBar, DownloadProgress, ModelUpdatesPanel}`
- `policy-controls/{PolicyToggle, RateLimitSection, SpendingLimitSection, AutoApproveSection, TimeWindowSection, ApprovedAddressesSection}`

## Root cause

These components reach `@elizaos/core` via `state/TranslationContext` → `state/persistence` (`import { logger } from "@elizaos/core"`). Two layered problems:

1. **Vite's dependency optimizer ignores `resolve.alias`.** It resolves bare `@elizaos/core` via `package.json` `exports` → the **Node** build (`src/index.node.ts`), not the browser entry. (A `resolve.alias` to `index.browser.ts` fixes on-demand transforms but not the pre-bundle pass.)
2. The Node entry pulls `features/plugin-manager`, which (a) calls `process.cwd()` at module-eval (`pluginAction = createPluginAction()`), and (b) imports `fs-extra`/`graceful-fs` that monkey-patch `fs.close` at eval. The patch throws `Cannot set property close … which has only a getter` against a read-only ESM `fs` stub (and `String(symbol)` inside Vite's `browser-external` proxy throws the misleading `Cannot convert a Symbol value to a string`).

## Already tried (clears the first two error classes, not the third)

- `@elizaos/core` → `index.browser.ts` alias (on-demand only)
- Node-builtin stub in **both** a Vite plugin and the optimizer's esbuild pass (proven `Reflect.get`-deferring proxy — fixes the Symbol-coercion class)
- Ambient `process` shim via preview `<head>` (fixes the `process.cwd` class)
- `optimizeDeps.exclude` of workspace pkgs — **regressed** `RunningAppsRow`, didn't sever fs-extra
- esbuild `onResolve` marking `@elizaos/core` external — didn't sever fs-extra (the scanner still discovers it)

`fs-extra` keeps reaching the optimizer graph through core's Node entry.

## Proposed fixes (any one)

1. **(preferred)** An `optimizeDeps.esbuildOptions` `onResolve` that maps `@elizaos/core` (and subpaths) to `index.browser.ts` **as a path** (not `external`), so the optimizer scanner crawls the browser graph (no `plugin-manager` → no `fs-extra`). Untried; the precise counter to "optimizer ignores `resolve.alias`".
2. A mutable (CJS) `fs` stub that `graceful-fs` can patch without throwing.
3. Decouple `state/persistence` from a *value* import of `@elizaos/core` — import `logger` from a lighter, browser-safe entry.

## Acceptance

The 11 stories above render in `storybook dev` (headless Playwright probe), and `bun run --cwd packages/ui lint && typecheck` stay clean.

topIssues.title: plugin-local-inference: voice→entity binding (bindEntity) is never called at runtime — recognized speakers never persist to the relationship graph
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

In `plugin-local-inference`'s voice pipeline, the plumbing to tie a recognized voice to an elizaOS `Entity` is fully present, but the **bind step appears to be unreachable at runtime** — `VoiceProfileStore.bindEntity` / `unbindEntity` have no caller outside tests. As a result a profile's `entityId` stays `null` in practice: attribution can resolve *which voice* is speaking, but the agent never persists *who that is* into the relationship graph.

> Grep-level finding on current `develop` — happy to be corrected if there's a dynamic/registered path I missed.

## What already works (so this isn't a rebuild)

- Voice profiles carry and persist an `entityId` — `plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/voice/profile-store.ts` (`bindEntity` L605, `unbindEntity` L627).
- Attribution resolves the entity on recognition — `src/services/voice/speaker/attribution-pipeline.ts:199` (`entityId: refined?.entityId ?? match.profile.entityId ?? null`).
- The UI already crowns the OWNER when the speaking voice's entity matches the owner — `packages/ui/.../ChatVoiceStatusBar` (`speakerEntityId === ownerEntityId`).

## The gap

`grep -rn bindEntity` across `plugins/` + `packages/` returns only the definitions in `profile-store.ts` and references in `__tests__/voice-profile-store.test.ts`. The voice-profile routes (`src/services/voice/voice-profile-routes.ts`) expose `activate` / list / soft-delete, but **no bind/claim endpoint**, and there is no agent action that binds. So there is no runtime path (route, action, or UI affordance) for "this voice = this entity."

Consequence: `entityId` stays `null`, the OWNER-crown only lights up in tests, and recognized speaker identity never reaches the relationship graph — the agent hears a known voice but can't remember *who* across sessions.

## Suggested shape (open to direction)

1. A bind/unbind route alongside the existing voice-profile routes, e.g. `POST /v1/voice/profiles/:id/bind { entityId }`.
2. An agent action so the agent can bind by voice ("this is <name>"), mirroring how `GENERATE_MEDIA` is wired.
3. On bind, route through `EntityStore` / `RelationshipStore` + the identity merge engine (per the root `CLAUDE.md` rule — don't bypass it), so the bound voice becomes a first-class relationship node.

Happy to take a first cut if this is wanted and not already in progress.

topPRs.title: feat(cloud): add shared runtime provisioning path
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- add execution-tier aware provisioning so shared Eliza agents run through the shared runtime without container provisioning
- wire create/provision/lifecycle/bridge routing around shared vs container-backed execution tiers
- add the ComputeProvider seam, DigitalOcean provider implementation, billing exclusions for shared agents, and cloud E2E coverage for shared/custom/lifecycle/dashboard flows

## Behind-base impact
- `origin/develop` did overlap with our edits in `packages/cloud-api/v1/coding-containers/route.ts`, `packages/cloud-shared/src/db/repositories/agent-billing.ts`, `packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts`, and `packages/ui/src/App.tsx`
- the full local branch also carried older UI/desktop commits that conflicted with current `develop`, so this PR is the focused cloud provisioning slice on top of `fd73d4b6f2`
- the only semantic conflict in the focused slice was `eliza-sandbox.ts`; it is resolved by preserving upstream `buildAgentInsertData` and threading `executionTier`/status through that helper

## Validation
- `bunx @biomejs/biome check --write <touched files>` passed; existing optional-chain suggestions remain in `packages/test/cloud-mocks/src/control-plane/server.ts`
- `bun test packages/cloud-api/__tests__/agent-bridge-runtime-routing.test.ts` passed, 2/2
- `git diff --check origin/develop..HEAD` passed
- conflict-marker scan over touched cloud paths passed

## Local validation caveat
- The original cloud branch with this focused code passed `bun run --cwd packages/test/cloud-e2e test` 18/18, `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend audit:cloud` 116/116, and `bun run verify` 460/460 before rebasing.
- In the rebased PR worktree, broader package typechecks/tests are blocked by local dependency hydration: `bun install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts` reports the lockfile would change, symlinked `node_modules` is missing required packages, and a detached verification checkout hit `No space left on device` before install. CI should re-run the full lane from a clean checkout.

topPRs.title: feat(plugin-calendar): extract first-class calendar plugin from lifeops; wire Odysseus (#8203)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Extracts the **entire** calendar surface out of `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops` into a new first-class **`@elizaos/plugin-calendar`**, keeps LifeOps fully working via thin wrappers + delegation, and wires the Odysseus `CalendarView` to the canonical calendar API (resolves #8203).

## What now lives in `@elizaos/plugin-calendar`
- **`CalendarService`** (former 1209-line mixin) + **`CalendarRepository`** + **drizzle schema** — same `app_lifeops.life_calendar_*` tables (no data migration).
- **Apple bridge**, **Google delegates**, **normalize helpers**.
- **Client methods** (`api/client-calendar.ts`, side-effect augmentation of the `@elizaos/ui` `ElizaClient`).
- **HTTP routes** — `handleCalendarRoutes` in `routes/calendar-routes.ts` (paths unchanged: `/api/lifeops/calendar/*`).
- **CALENDAR action + ~4280-line LLM handler** — `createCalendarActionRunner(deps)` + `extractCalendarPlanWithLlm`.
- **Dashboard UI** — `CalendarSection`, `EventEditorDrawer`, `useCalendarWeek` (exported via a server-graph-free `./ui` barrel).
- **Contract types** in `@elizaos/shared/contracts/calendar` (the only layer both `@elizaos/ui` and the plugins can depend on without a cycle).

## The seam (no dependency cycle — strictly `lifeops → calendar`)
Cross-domain concerns calendar can't own are **injected** from thin LifeOps wrappers (mirrors the documented plugin-health owner-action pattern):
- `CalendarHostGate` — Google connector grants + reminder-plan/audit persistence (default gate resolves Google directly + no-ops reminders; LifeOps injects the real impl at init).
- `CalendarRouteDeps` — LifeOps' HTTP plumbing (telemetry/schema-bootstrap, rate limiting, body parsing).
- `CalendarActionDeps` — trajectory LLM model runners, recent-conversation context, optional travel-time buffer.
- `CalendarSectionProps` — dashboard-shell deps (chat launcher, selection, event priming).

LifeOps retains only thin wrappers (delegation shim for the service methods, gate impl, the `CALENDAR` owner-action wrapper with `hasLifeOpsAccess` gating, the `LifeOpsCalendarSection` wrapper) — plus the legitimate cross-domain consumers (reminders/google reading the calendar cache).

## Odysseus (#8203)
`CalendarView` overlays a **read-only provider feed** (Google/Apple via the calendar client) on top of the existing local-first calendars + `.ics` (the **augment** option). Stale header fixed.

## Verification (all green)
- `@elizaos/plugin-calendar`: typecheck (0 errors) + build + **31 tests** (Apple bridge, normalize/merge units, real-PGlite `CalendarService` CRUD asserting persistence, feed aggregation, next-event context, and gate reminder-plan side effects).
- `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops`: full build (js + **views bundle** + types) + 53 calendar-touching unit tests.
- `@elizaos/plugin-task-coordinator`: build + 25 tests. `@elizaos/shared`: 777 tests + typecheck. `@elizaos/ui`: typecheck.
- No `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops` import anywhere in `plugin-calendar` (cycle-free).

## Follow-up (noted, low-harm)
A few calendar-specific formatters/delegates were copied into plugin-calendar and are now unused inside LifeOps' shared `google/format-helpers.ts` / `google-plugin-delegates.ts`; surgical removal from those multi-export files was deferred.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): operator inspector and recovery controls
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

This draft branch integrates the orchestrator/operator-inspector work into the elizaOS app and agent surfaces so it can be reviewed, tested, and selectively harvested. It adds richer orchestrator task APIs, operator recovery controls, edited-plan restart flow, timeline/message/usage plumbing, typed UI client coverage, and a workbench-style UI for inspecting tasks and sub-agent sessions.

This is intentionally a **draft**. The branch proves the end-to-end contracts and test coverage, but the UI should be reviewed critically before anything is treated as final visual/product direction.

## What changed

- Extended the orchestrator API surface for task detail, task lists, messages, events, timeline, usage, plan revisions, pause/resume, archive/reopen, fork, validate, retry, rerun, restart, and restart-with-edited-plan flows.
- Hardened orchestrator task service/store/mapper behavior around richer operator state, including sessions, artifacts, plan revisions, usage, active task/session counts, recovery state, and persisted task metadata.
- Expanded `OrchestratorWorkbench` with an operator inspector, task rail, sub-agent session cards, message/timeline rendering, plan/acceptance panels, recovery controls, plan editor, and message composer wiring.
- Added typed client methods and contract coverage for the new orchestrator API shapes in `packages/ui`.
- Stabilized app-shell route loading and smoke/live-stack behavior for `/`, `/chat`, `/orchestrator`, `/apps/tasks`, app routes, and settings/connectors paths.
- Added an env-gated local demo fixture mode for the smoke API stub: `ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR=1`. This lets reviewers open the normal app locally and see a populated orchestrator task with demo sub-agents without needing real provider credentials.
- Added detailed and simple HTML docs describing the branch, status, vision, validation, and how the orchestrator/operator inspector fits into elizaOS.

## Reviewer note for Shaw / agent review

The useful review path is probably:

1. Start with `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/api/orchestrator-routes.ts` and the matching service/store changes to verify the backend contract and recovery semantics.
2. Check `packages/ui/src/api/client-agent.ts` and `client-types-cloud.ts` to confirm the frontend contract matches the route behavior.
3. Review `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx` for product/UX direction, but treat this part as rough draft UI rather than final polish.
4. Review the smoke fixture in `packages/app-core/scripts/playwright-ui-smoke-api-stub.mjs` as local test/demo support only. It is behind `ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR=1` and should not affect default smoke behavior.
5. Use the tests below to decide which parts are ready to keep versus split or refine.

## Local demo command

To see the populated local orchestrator surface without real agents/providers:

```bash
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_SKIP_BUILD=1 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_FORCE_STUB=1 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR=1 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_API_PORT=31338 \
ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_PORT=2144 \
node packages/app-core/scripts/run-node-tsx.mjs packages/app-core/scripts/playwright-ui-live-stack.ts
```

Then open:

- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/orchestrator`
- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/apps/tasks`
- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/chat`
- `http://127.0.0.1:2144/`

The demo fixture seeds a task named `Build Kanban planner app` with `Codex Builder` and `Cerebras Reviewer` sessions, usage counters, timeline/messages, plan state, and operator controls.

## Validation performed

- `packages/app` lint, typecheck, and build passed.
- `packages/ui` typecheck and build passed.
- `packages/agent` lint, typecheck, and build passed.
- `plugin-agent-orchestrator` unit tests/build passed.
- `plugin-task-coordinator` unit tests/build passed.
- `plugin-app-manager` tests/build passed.
- App route/view/smoke coverage tests passed.
- Selector-based local route sweep passed for `/`, `/chat`, `/orchestrator`, `/apps/skills`, `/apps/trajectories`, `/apps/tasks`, and `/settings#connectors`.
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e test/ui-smoke/orchestrator-gui-workbench.spec.ts` passed.
- Live demo stack was verified with Playwright: opened `/orchestrator`, selected the seeded task, confirmed the sub-agent inspector, sent a prompt through the composer, and confirmed the demo orchestrator response rendered in the message list.

Notes: app build still emits existing Vite dynamic-import warnings, and agent lint still emits existing warnings in `conversation-routes.ts`. Those were not introduced by this branch.

## Known caveats

- The UI is draft-quality and should not be merged as final visual design without product/design review.
- The local demo fixture is not a real agent runner; it is an env-gated stub for review and smoke testing.
- This is a broad branch. It may be worth splitting before merge into backend contract/service changes, UI workbench changes, smoke/test harness changes, and docs.
- No PR merge is requested from this branch as-is; this draft exists so reviewers can inspect the work and decide what to keep.

topPRs.title: fix(dev): reliable agent hot-reload via source-only watcher
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
The dev API child ran with watch off by default (and auto-disabled whenever a concurrent build was detected), so agent/plugin source edits never reached the running agent. `node --watch` followed imports into dist/ and hot-reloaded mid-build, which is why it had to stay disabled.

Replace it with a source-only watcher (packages/*/src + plugins/*/src, excluding frontend packages, dist, node_modules, and tests) that bounces the supervised API child on change — decoupled from dist churn, so concurrent builds never trigger it. Add an intentional restart() to the API supervisor that relaunches immediately without counting toward the crash-loop streak. On by default; opt out with ELIZA_DEV_NO_WATCH=1.

completedItems.title: chore(deps): bump hono 4.12.18 -> 4.12.21 across cloud packages
completedItems.body: Supersedes #8239 (Dependabot). That PR bumped `hono` 4.12.18 → 4.12.21 in 8 `package.json`s **but did not update `bun.lock`**, so `bun install --frozen-lockfile` failed (`lockfile had changes, but lockfile is frozen`) and it could not merge
